notes: Crashing every 3-5 hours is the only hangup after tinkering was done
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered

notes:
Took me on and off for 3 weeks to get the game running. 
These ingame settings helped me be able to run the game with no crashes. (Also launch command)
Fullscreen
Quality level Medium (Low makes the game run worse??)
Lumen Hardware RT = Off
Lumen Software  = Low
Upscaling DLSS
DLSS Mode = Performance
Nvidia Reflex = Off

The game still runs pretty bad, but at least it doesnt crash. On windows I could have most things on high with better FPS. I don't understand the platinum rating :(
title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered

title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion Remastered

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT (radeonsi, gfx1201, ACO, DRM 3.64, 6.18.6-200.fc43.x86_64)
notes:
The game runs just fine, but every time there is an update something with the shader cache causes the game to crash. The menus load but once you actually start to play the game freezes immediately. I believe it has something to do with both DLSS and FSR as I have had this happen on both my NVIDIA GPU and my AMD GPU. Disabling upscaling the game will run.

I have found uninstalling and reinstalling the game fixes the issue. I am sure there is an easier way to do it, maybe delete everything from the shader cache in the Steam files and delete mesa cache. It's annoying for sure.

I like playing this game, but it seems to give me far more issues than any of the other games I play on Linux.
